extraction.one_line_summary: FERRON Valentin attacked inside final kilometer from 6-rider breakaway, won as experienced riders (Baguio, Rolland, Bagioli) looked at each other instead of chasing
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step sent BAGIOLI in break despite being 3:02 down on GC, forcing Jumbo-Visma to control behind entire stage
Teams successfully eliminated Groenewegen using high tempo and crosswinds before finale, prioritizing rival removal over breakaway control
Experienced breakaway riders (Baguio, Rolland, Bagioli) hesitated when 24-year-old attacked final kilometer, letting unknown beat World Tour winners

rider_signals.BAGIOLI Andrea: positive: Quick-Step used him as breakaway GC threat at -3:02 to pin Jumbo-Visma, tactical sacrifice for Evenepoel
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: Teams successfully collaborated with high tempo and crosswinds to eliminate him before finale again, clear targeting pattern
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: positive: Domestique work at 5km despite having won stages himself shows complete team commitment in yellow jersey defense
rider_signals.FERRON Valentin: positive: 24-year-old recognized experienced riders' hesitation psychology and attacked final kilometer when others had superior palmares
